title: Error when pulling an image with Podman 5.3.2 on windows: tls: bad record MAC
body:
### Issue Description

I have installed podman 5.3.2, created a machine with WSL on Windows and tried to pull an image, I got an error at the end of image pull: `Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: storing blob to file "/var/tmp/container_images_storage2134780752/1": happened during read: local error: tls: bad record MAC`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman 5.3.2 from setup.exe
2. Create podman machine and start it, rootful
3. Pull some image


### Describe the results you received

```
PS C:\Users\Ondrej> podman pull docker.io/library/alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:1f3e46996e2966e4faa5846e56e76e3748b7315e2ded61476c24403d592134f0
Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: storing blob to file "/var/tmp/container_images_storage2134780752/1": happened during read: local error: tls: bad record MAC
PS C:\Users\Ondrej> podman machine ls
NAME                    VM TYPE     CREATED             LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default  wsl         About a minute ago  Currently running  4           2GiB        100GiB
```

### Describe the results you expected

Image was pulled without an error.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10 Pro, x64, WSL provider.

### Additional information

comments.body:
I tried to reinstall podman with 5.3.1 and all works with older version. 

Update: some images throws the error. So 5.3.1 did not fix the problem.

@Luap99 please close the issue... 

Reasons: 
* pull image tests works well on CI (for podman desktop)
* tested on different machine without issue
* confirmed by another person there is no such issue 

I switched to a different WiFi SSID (from 2.4 Ghz to 5Ghz) and the problem was resolved. I reinstalled firmware on my router and everything works well with 5.3.2 on 2.4 Ghz wifi now. So the problem was in my wifi connection (I do not know what exactly).
